Title: Content Scheduling — Start Date
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Click Edit to open the editing form
4. Set Start Date to a future date and time (e.g., tomorrow at 09:00)
5. Leave End Date empty
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify start date is saved and displayed correctly
8. Verify content is NOT visible on storefront before the start date
9. Advance system time or wait for start date and verify content becomes visible
Expected Result:
- Start Date field accepts a valid future date and time
- Published content is inactive and not shown on storefront before the start date
- After the start date is reached the content becomes active automatically
- Start date and time are displayed correctly in the content publishing list
- Is Active flag is automatically managed based on date range

Title: Content Scheduling — End Date

Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing with Is Active = TRUE exists
Steps:

3. Click Edit
4. Set End Date to a past date and time
5. Save the publishing
6. Verify the content publishing is now inactive or expired
7. Check storefront to confirm content is no longer visible
8. Set End Date to a future date and save
9. Verify content becomes visible on storefront again
Expected Result:

- Setting End Date to a past date deactivates the content publishing
- Content is no longer rendered on the storefront after the end date
- Setting a future End Date re-enables content visibility
- Expired content publishing shows appropriate status in the list

Title: Content Scheduling — Date Range
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Content publishing exists [MKT-010]
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open an existing content publishing
3. Click Edit
4. Set Start Date to a past date (e.g., yesterday)
5. Set End Date to a future date (e.g., next week)
6. Save the publishing
7. Verify content is currently visible on storefront (within the date range)
8. Update End Date to a past date and save
9. Verify content is no longer visible on storefront
10. Verify that Start Date before End Date validation is enforced
Expected Result:
- Both Start Date and End Date can be set simultaneously
- Content is visible only when the current date is within the defined range
- Setting End Date earlier than Start Date triggers a validation error
- Content automatically deactivates when End Date is reached
- Date range is clearly displayed in the content publishing details

Title: Promotion — Usage Limit
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Promotion with coupon exists [MKT-061]
Steps:

3. Edit the coupon and set Maximum uses number to 2

7. Attempt to use the same coupon code again with User1
8. Verify the per-user limit is enforced
9. Use the coupon code with User2 to consume the global limit
10. Attempt a third usage and verify it is blocked
Expected Result:
- Maximum uses number limits total redemptions across all users
- Maximum uses per user limits redemptions for a single user
- Coupon is accepted when within usage limits
- Second usage by the same user is rejected with an appropriate error message
- Coupon is blocked for all users after the global maximum uses is reached

Admin logged in to Backend
Promotion exists [MKT-021]

Title: Promotion — Stacking Rules
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Two active promotions exist with different effects
CombineStackable policy is set in Settings
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/promotions
2. Set Promotion1 as "Can be combined" (combinable)
3. Set Promotion2 as "Can be combined" (combinable)
4. On storefront verify both promotions are applied simultaneously
5. Set Promotion2 as "Exclusive" (cannot be combined)
6. Trigger both promotions on storefront
7. Verify only the exclusive promotion is applied
8. Set both promotions as exclusive
9. Verify only the higher-priority promotion is applied
10. Verify the stacking rule behavior matches the CombineStackable policy
Expected Result:
- When both promotions are combinable, both discounts are applied to the cart
- When one promotion is exclusive, only that exclusive promotion is applied
- When both are exclusive, only the promotion with higher priority is applied
- Stacking behavior is consistent with the policy configured in Settings
- The applied and excluded promotions are clearly indicated in the cart summary

Title: xAPI Marketing Content Query — Query Active Content via GraphQL
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Active content publishing exists with Is Active = TRUE
xAPI endpoint accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent and ensure content publishing is active
2. Open GraphQL playground or API client targeting the xAPI endpoint
3. Query for dynamic content items using the appropriate xAPI query
4. Verify the response includes the active content item
5. Set Is Active = FALSE on the content publishing in admin
6. Re-execute the GraphQL query
7. Verify the response does not include the now-inactive content
8. Re-enable Is Active = TRUE and verify content returns in query
9. Verify query results match the admin configuration exactly
Expected Result:
- xAPI GraphQL query returns active content items when Is Active = TRUE
- Query response contains correct content item data: name, type, content body
- Setting Is Active = FALSE removes the content from xAPI query results
- Re-enabling Is Active = TRUE restores the content item in query results
- Query response time is within acceptable performance thresholds
- No unauthorized content items appear in the response

Title: Search in Publishing List View — Use Search Bar to Find Published Content
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Multiple content publishings exist with different names
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/marketing/dynamicContent
2. Open the Content Publishing list
3. Click on the search bar
4. Enter a partial name of an existing content publishing
5. Verify the list filters to show matching results
6. Enter the full exact name and verify single result
7. Enter a name that does not match any publishing
8. Verify the list shows empty state or no results message
9. Clear the search field and verify the full list is restored
Expected Result:
- Search bar is visible and functional in the content publishing list view
- Entering a partial name filters the list to show matching content publishings
- Entering the exact name returns the specific content publishing
- Non-matching search term shows empty state with appropriate message
- Clearing the search field restores the complete unfiltered list
- Search is case-insensitive and returns consistent results
